senators.name: Roger Wicker
senators.notNerdReason: B.A. and J.D. from University of Mississippi. Air Force officer. Career as attorney, state senator, and U.S. Representative before Senate. Long-tenured with deep expertise on Armed Services and Commerce committees. Solid policy knowledge but approach is more institutional than analytically driven.
education: J.D., University of Mississippi School of Law

senators.name: Cindy Hyde-Smith
senators.notNerdReason: A.A. from Copiah-Lincoln Community College and B.A. from University of Southern Mississippi. Career as cattle rancher, state senator, and Mississippi Commissioner of Agriculture and Commerce. Agricultural and political background without analytical or evidence-based policy approach.

MS-01.name: Trent Kelly
MS-01.notNerdReason: B.A. and J.D. from University of Mississippi. Army National Guard Brigadier General. Career as district attorney and engineer in private practice. Military leadership at general officer level requires strategic analytical capability. Engineering background provides technical skills. However, legislative approach not distinctly evidence-based or data-driven.
education: J.D., University of Mississippi School of Law

MS-02.name: Bennie Thompson
MS-02.notNerdReason: B.A. from Tougaloo College and M.S. from Jackson State University. Longest-serving member of Mississippi's delegation. Former Chairman of the House Homeland Security Committee and chairman of the January 6th Select Committee. Deep institutional expertise in homeland security policy. Strong policy depth but approach is more experienced-based than analytically driven.
experience: Former Chairman, House Homeland Security Committee

MS-03.name: Michael Guest
MS-03.notNerdReason: B.A. from Mississippi State University and J.D. from University of Mississippi School of Law. Career as prosecutor (District Attorney for 20th Judicial District). Legal training provides analytical foundation but governance approach is more prosecutorial than evidence-based.
education: J.D., University of Mississippi School of Law

MS-04.name: Mike Ezell
MS-04.notNerdReason: B.A. from William Carey University. Career as sheriff of Jackson County for 8 years. Law enforcement background. No analytical or evidence-based policy approach.
